target: Prose fixes in §4.2.3: add forward-link to §4.2.7; OBE-dismiss ISS-1623, ISS-1723, ISS-1661

obe_resolutions.reason: OBE: sentence already split in current docs/index.html. The §4.2.3 prepend reads as two distinct sentences with no semicolon splice (verified at line 3007). The curmudgeon reviewed the proposal text, but the decider applied the already-corrected form on integration.

OBE: Hartland 88% obscuration already cited at docs/index.html line 3089 — cites NASA SE2026Aug12T Besselian elements, Fred Espenak / NASA GSFC, with per-station link to eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov. Citation is present and complete.

OBE: Canary poller actively monitors for dome author fixing the OTS wrong-side error (monitor/prompts/poller.md lines 118-134 define the canary trigger). When the dome author separates prediction and observation documents and timestamps them independently, the poller flags it as critical with analyst_priority HIGH. The tile staleness concern is covered by the existing canary monitoring pipeline — no additional tracking mechanism needed.

patches.description: Add forward-link to §4.2.7 at end of §4.2.3 closing paragraph (ISS-1625)
patches.find_pattern: The prediction is structured so that almost any physically plausible quiet-day result is claimed as confirmation.
patches.replace_pattern: The prediction is structured so that almost any physically plausible quiet-day result is claimed as confirmation. (A second, independent failure is documented in §4.2.7: the dome's own published Sun and Moon altitudes produce a totality path roughly 1.3 km wide — not the 294‑km observed path.)

tldr_evidence: Three OBE dismissals (sentence already split, citation already present, canary poller already tracks). One active prose patch: adds a forward-link from §4.2.3 closing sentence to §4.2.7, as the curmudgeon recommended when reviewing EXP-253.
tldr_verdict: Net change: one forward-link sentence appended to §4.2.3; three ISS dismissed as already-resolved.
